title: Mighty Algerian Chicken Pie
description: A rich, gooey post-workout Algerian pie with spiced chicken, fresh spinach, and a buttery shortcrust, all in a low-sodium-friendly en-croute.
instructions: In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté onion and garlic until translucent.
Add diced carrots and cook for 5 minutes, then stir in harissa paste, cumin, and paprika, cooking until fragrant.
Add chicken pieces, cook until no longer pink, then fold in spinach and lemon juice, cooking until spinach wilts.
Transfer the filling to a bowl and let it cool slightly.
Roll out the shortcrust pastry on a lightly floured surface and cut into two equal halves.
Spoon the filling onto one pastry half, brush the edges with beaten egg, then top with the second pastry piece, sealing edges with a fork.
Brush the pie crust with remaining beaten egg and make a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake for 35 minutes, until the crust is golden and the filling is bubbling; let rest 10 minutes before serving.
